% import-test.tex -- imports the standalone problem with all its inputs FIXED via
% \importproblem's override argument, so the rendered values are deterministic; a
% following question then reads \get{leak} to prove the import's own variables did
% not leak past pop_scope (it must read undefined, i.e. "??").
\documentclass{autoexam}
\versions{A}
\examsetup{number=1, date={Engine Test}}

\begin{document}
\begin{problems}
	\question
	\importproblem{import-problem.tex}{a=3, b=4, c=5}
	\question
	AFTERIMP \get{leak} ENDAFTER
\end{problems}
\end{document}
